channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Here's your guide to voting in Texas's May 2025 local elections
item.description: On May 3, many Texans will vote on local leaders and initiatives. Here's how to check for elections in your area and register by April 3.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By María Méndez And Yuriko Schumacher, KPRC 2

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada is testing Republican support
item.description: With President Donald Trump’s so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The key places to watch in Tuesday's elections in Wisconsin and Florida
item.description: A handful of elections in Florida and Wisconsin have become key tests of President Donald Trump’s political standing just over two months into his second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Robert Yoon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida Republicans face off against well-funded Democrats in US House special elections
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine and chief financial officer Jimmy Patronis are looking to win their congressional special elections.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Entire staff at federal agency that funds libraries and museums put on leave
item.description: The staff of the Institute of Museum and Library Services was placed on administrative leave Monday morning, following a meeting between IMLS leadership and DOGE staff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration says it deported 17 more ‘violent criminals’ to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said Monday that it has deported 17 more “violent criminals” from the Tren de Aragua and MS-13 gangs to El Salvador, as it doubles down on a policy of removing people from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MARCOS ALEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawley officially a yes on Dr. Oz after securing commitments on transgender, abortion issues
item.description: Hawley said he was delighted that Dr. Oz responded to his inquiry and walked back previous promotion of transgender and abortion isses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Adams calls on judge to ‘promptly’ dismiss bribery case, citing campaign
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) on Monday called on the federal judge mulling whether to toss his criminal bribery charges to act with urgency as a deadline approaches later this week for mayoral candidates to submit their candidate petitions. For weeks, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pardons Navy veteran convicted in Capitol riot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has pardoned a Virginia man whose sentence already was commuted for his convictions stemming from the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic base's anger puts some party leaders on shaky ground
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Democratic base is angry. Not just at President Donald Trump, Elon Musk and the “Make America Great Again” movement. Rank-and-file Democrats are mad at their own leaders and increasingly agitating to replace them. Democrats in Arizona and Georgia pushed out their party chairs.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee bill to requires schools to teach ‘success sequence’ of life path
item.description: The "Success Sequence Act" would require schools to teach the positive outcomes associated with completing the following in the following order: graduate high school, enter the workforce or go to college, get married, and then have children.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump faces crucial week on the economy
item.description: President Trump is entering a critical week for the economy amid growing fears that his penchant for tariffs could stall growth and undercut progress on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Historic tree to be cut down at the White House over safety concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A tree that's nearly two centuries old will be removed from the White House grounds because it's deteriorating, President Donald Trump said Sunday. The southern magnolia stands near the curved portico on the south side of the building.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term as president
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term. “I'm not joking,” he says in an NBC News interview.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair opens investigation into Disney and ABC over DEI practices
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr said he wants a review all of Disney's DEI practices — both past and present — to determine whether the company has ever violated any FCC regulations.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Schumer, Democrats seek to get off the mat amid new Trump controversy
item.description: Democrats and their embattled Senate Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) have received a welcome change to rebound amid the controversy surrounding the Signal group chat that entangled a number of Trump Cabinet members over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts, vying to exploit the GOP’s advised moratorium on the events to make inroads with frustrated voters, pick up battleground seats, and flip control of the House in next year’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Media questions can shape Trump's decisions in second term
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota food shelves worried as USDA cancels truckloads of food
item.description: This week, the U.S. Department of Agriculture canceled almost half of food shelf orders from Minnesota farmers under the federal Commodity Credit Corporation. Organizations providing nutrition programs say they are concerned about how to provide for their clientele.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Musk promises to go to Wisconsin to personally deliver $2 million to voters in Supreme Court race
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk says he plans to hold a rally in Wisconsin to “personally hand over” $2 million to a pair of voters who have already cast their ballots in the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5
